mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, signals a healthy and predictable economic environment. This reduces uncertainty for businesses and investors, supporting higher corporate valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a deteriorating labor market. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicates vigorous consumer demand and investment activity. This joint strength boosts corporate earnings expectations across various sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a rapidly rising FEDFUNDS rate increases the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, while also reducing overall market liquidity. This leads to lower equity valuations as discount rates rise and future earnings are pressured, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during a clear monetary tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This fundamental strength typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, which in turn supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and broader supply-side activity. This indicates weakening economic fundamentals and reduced corporate output, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from declining industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A strong labor market, characterized by low unemployment (UNRATE) combined with robust employment growth (PAYEMS), suggests strong consumer purchasing power and confidence. This leads to increased aggregate demand and higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction between UNRATE and PAYEMS is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, persistent high CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates aggressive monetary policy to curb inflation. This typically leads to an economic slowdown, higher borrowing costs, and reduced corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during a high inflation and aggressive monetary tightening regime.
